Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given event logs for 4 orders case_id 1001 1002 1003 1004 and we are to identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors. We are also to hypothesize why these anomalies might occur and propose SQL queries to investigate further. Steps 1. We note the assumed normal process flow as per the prompt is Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment. 2. Lets examine each case Case 1001 Follows the normal flow exactly. No anomaly. Case 1002 Events 1. Register Order 2. Confirm Shipment without prior Validate Stock or Perform Credit Check 3. Ship Goods 4. Perform Credit Check after shipping 5. Validate Stock after shipping 6. Issue Invoice 7. Receive Payment Anomaly The order was shipped before credit check and stock validation. This is a serious process violation. Case 1003 Events 1. Register Order 2. Perform Credit Check 3. Ship Goods without prior Validate Stock or Confirm Shipment 4. Issue Invoice 5. Confirm Shipment after shipping 6. Receive Payment Anomaly The order was shipped without stock validation and the shipment confirmation happened after shipping. Also note that Confirm Shipment is after Ship Goods which is out of order. Case 1004 Events 1. Register Order 2. Receive Payment immediately after order registration without any other steps 3. Issue Invoice 4. Confirm Shipment with shipment_scheduled N meaning not scheduled 5. Ship Goods Anomalies Payment was received before invoice was issued and without credit check stock validation and shipment confirmation. The shipment confirmation has additional_info shipment_scheduled N which might indicate that the shipment was not scheduled but then it was shipped.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 3. Hypotheses for the anomalies For Case 1002 Possibly the steps were done in a rush for a priority order order_type is priority and the process was expedited without proper checks. Alternatively there might be a system error that allowed the steps to be recorded out of order. For Case 1003 The warehouse might have shipped the goods without checking stock leading to a potential stock issue. The late confirmation of shipment might be due to a delay in updating the system. For Case 1004 This could be a pre-paid order but note that the normal flow still requires credit check and stock validation before shipping. The payment was received very early which might indicate a different process for pre-paid orders but then the credit check is still required The shipment_scheduled N in Confirm Shipment but then shipped might indicate an override or error.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. We are to propose SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses further. We note that we have three tables order_event_log orders and resources. Possible queries a. For Case 1002 priority order Check if there are other priority orders that had similar issues. b. For each case we might want to check the time differences between events to see if there are unusual time gaps. c. Check if the same resource is involved in multiple anomalies. d. Check for orders that are missing required steps like credit check or have steps in the wrong order. e. For Case 1004 check if there are other orders that received payment before other steps. We can write queries to List all cases that have a step performed out of order according to the normal flow. For each case check if the normal sequence of activities is followed. However note that the normal flow is linear and we can assign a sequence number to each activity in the normal flow. Lets assign Register Order 1 Perform Credit Check 2 Validate Stock 3 Confirm Shipment 4 Ship Goods 5 Issue Invoice 6 Receive Payment 7 Then for each event in a case we can assign the expected sequence number and then compare with the actual sequence of events. We can do this by WITH normal_flow AS SELECT Register Order AS activity 1 AS seq UNION ALL SELECT Perform Credit Check 2 UNION ALL SELECT Validate Stock 3 UNION ALL SELECT Confirm Shipment 4 UNION ALL SELECT Ship Goods 5 UNION ALL SELECT Issue Invoice 6 UNION ALL SELECT Receive Payment 7 case_events AS SELECT case_id activity timestamp ROW_NUMBER OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS actual_seq FROM order_event_log SELECT ce.case_id ce.activity ce.timestamp ce.actual_seq nf.seq AS expected_seq FROM case_events ce JOIN normal_flow nf ON ce.activity nf.activity ORDER BY ce.case_id ce.actual_seq But note the same activity might appear multiple times in a case In our data each activity appears once per case. Alternatively we can check for each case if the sequence of activities matches the expected sequence. However note that the expected sequence is linear and we are not accounting for parallel activities. But in our example we assume the process is linear.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can then look for cases where the actual sequence of activities does not match the expected sequence. Another approach For each case we can list the activities in the order they occurred and then compare with the normal order. We can also check for missing steps. Lets break down the queries Query 1 Find cases where the normal sequence is violated steps out of order. We can use the above CTE to get the expected sequence for each activity and then check for each case if the activities are in increasing order of expected sequence. We can use WITH normal_flow AS case_events AS SELECT case_id FROM case_events ce JOIN normal_flow nf ON ce.activity nf.activity GROUP BY case_id HAVING MAX nf.seq != 7 This checks if the case has reached the last step Not exactly. But we want to check the order. We can use WITH normal_flow AS case_events AS SELECT ce.case_id ce.activity ce.actual_seq nf.seq AS expected_seq FROM case_events ce JOIN normal_flow nf ON ce.activity nf.activity WHERE ce.actual_seq != nf.seq ORDER BY ce.case_id ce.actual_seq This will show activities that are not in the expected position. But note if an activity is missing then the actual_seq will be off. Alternatively we can use WITH normal_flow AS case_events AS SELECT ce.case_id ce.activity ce.actual_seq nf.seq AS expected_seq LAG nf.seq OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY ce.actual_seq AS prev_expected_seq FROM case_events ce JOIN normal_flow nf ON ce.activity nf.activity Then for each event we can check if the expected sequence is increasing. But note if an activity is missing then the expected sequence might jump. We can also check for missing steps by comparing the set of activities in a case with the set of normal activities. Query 2 Check for missing steps in a case. We can do WITH normal_steps AS SELECT unnest ARRAY Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment AS activity case_activities AS SELECT DISTINCT case_id activity FROM order_event_log SELECT ns.activity ca.case_id FROM normal_steps ns LEFT JOIN case_activities ca ON ns.activity ca.activity WHERE ca.case_id IS NULL But this will show which steps are missing in which case Actually we want for each case which normal steps are missing. Alternatively WITH normal_steps AS SELECT unnest ARRAY Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment AS activity cases AS SELECT DISTINCT case_id FROM order_event_log SELECT c.case_id ns.activity FROM cases c CROSS JOIN normal_steps ns LEFT JOIN order_event_log oel ON c.case_id oel.case_id AND ns.activity oel.activity WHERE oel.case_id IS NULL Query 3 Check for orders that were shipped before credit check or stock validation. We can do WITH credit_check AS SELECT case_id timestamp AS credit_check_time FROM order_event_log WHERE activity Perform Credit Check stock_validation AS SELECT case_id timestamp AS stock_validation_time FROM order_event_log WHERE activity Validate Stock ship_goods AS SELECT case_id timestamp AS ship_time FROM order_event_log WHERE activity Ship Goods SELECT sg.case_id sg.ship_time cc.credit_check_time sv.stock_validation_time FROM ship_goods sg LEFT JOIN credit_check cc ON sg.case_id cc.case_id LEFT JOIN stock_validation sv ON sg.case_id sv.case_id WHERE sg.ship_time COALESCE cc.credit_check_time infinity timestamp OR sg.ship_time COALESCE sv.stock_validation_time infinity timestamp This will return cases where the ship time is before the credit check or stock validation. Query 4 Check for orders that received payment before invoice. WITH invoice AS SELECT case_id timestamp AS invoice_time FROM order_event_log WHERE activity Issue Invoice payment AS SELECT case_id timestamp AS payment_time FROM order_event_log WHERE activity Receive Payment SELECT p.case_id p.payment_time i.invoice_time FROM payment p LEFT JOIN invoice i ON p.case_id i.case_id WHERE p.payment_time i.invoice_time Query 5 Check for resources involved in anomalies. We can join the events with the resources table to see the role and department of the resources involved in out-of-order events. For example for the ship goods before credit check WITH anomalies AS Query 3 results SELECT a.case_id oel.resource r.role r.department FROM anomalies a JOIN order_event_log oel ON a.case_id oel.case_id AND oel.activity Ship Goods JOIN resources r ON oel.resource r.resource_id These are some initial queries. We can also check for time gaps between events but that might be more about efficiency than process violation.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Lets write the SQL queries for the above. However note the prompt says to propose SQL queries without any hints or guidance. We are to write the queries as if we are the LLM. We will write the queries in the answer. We are to present the anomalies hypotheses and then the SQL queries. Lets structure the answer Step 1 Identify anomalies in each case. Step 2 Hypothesize reasons. Step 3 Propose SQL queries to investigate.
